- draft (maybe) - submitted - final version ("camera ready" - with tagged [authorarchive](https://ctan.org/pkg/authorarchive) - or not) - publisher version (typest camera ready) - annotated When sharing with others, the "final version" is the one to share. Maybe publisher version Sometimes, one also has the conference-presentation. That could also be coleldcted. Our "File" field should explicitly support this. The file name convention, I use is to put the suffixes there - and leve the suffix out for "final version" --> The "File" field should have some magic. Main table should open anoted, final, publisher, submitted, draft

Closes #13570

1. Launch JabRef on linux
2. Check icon

summary: JabRef comment refers to "Tests" instead of "Source Code Tests"

description.text: ### JabRef version Latest development branch build (please note build date below) ### Operating system Other (please describe below) ### Details on version and operating system github actions through website ### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog) - [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version. - [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists ### Steps to reproduce the behaviour I received an email from JabRef Machine with this body: > You modified Markdown (*.md) files and did not meet JabRef's rules for consistently formatted Markdown files. To ensure consistent styling, we have [markdown-lint](https://github.com/DavidAnson/markdownlint) in place. [Markdown lint's rules](https://github.com/DavidAnson/markdownlint/blob/main/doc/Rules.md#rules) help to keep our Markdown files consistent within this repository and consistent with the Markdown files outside here. > > You can check the detailed error output by navigating to your pull request, selecting the tab "Checks", section "Tests" (on the left), subsection "Markdown". I appreciated the pointer to the test output; however, the section is "Source Code Tests", not "Tests". ### Appendix _No response_

Instead of the standard icon, the OpenJDK penguin icon appears after installation (for launch the application). 

When the application is launched, it is indicated by a separate gear icon on the dash bar.

summary: Automatic lookup of DOI at "Citation information"

description.text: In the tab "Citation information, if there is no DOI, offer a "link" to determine the DOI. 1. The text "The entry does not have a DOI" should be replaced by the functionlity at the tab "Citation relations" (See PR https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/13539)\ 2. When clicked, the text should change to "Looking up DOI..." 3. If no DOI found, notifiy users "No DOI found" and change to initial state. 4. If DOI found: Use it for lookin up information in this tab --- Add following paper to JabRef: ```bibtex @InProceedings{Edwards2004, author = {S. H. Edwards}, booktitle = {Proceedings of the 35th SIGCSE Technical Symposium on Computer Science Education}, date = {2004}, title = {Using software testing to move students from trial-and-error to reflection-in-action}, pages = {26--30}, } ``` Click on "Citation information" Now switch to "General" and click "Look up DOI" ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cccebd9d-8250-4446-922d-05161f56dcef) See that a DOI is dermined: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2ee9e301-3e62-42ee-8172-961e41b02741) Then, switch to onther entry (or create a new one). Swich back. Then, "Citation information" should work (if there was no network error)

### JabRef version 5.15 (latest release) ### Operating system Windows ### Details on version and operating system Windows 11 ### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog) - [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version. - [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists ### Steps to reproduce the behaviour 1. I open a "LibreOffice Writer" file 2. I insert a reference with JabRef 3. I place the cursor at the end of the reference 4. I press the "space button" => Problem : The reference is extended, so I can't add more text after the reference ### Appendix ...

summary: JabRef dialog doesn't respect font size

description.text:
### JabRef version Latest development branch build (please note build date below) ### Operating system Windows ### Details on version and operating system Windows 11 26100 ### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog) - [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version. - [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists ### Steps to reproduce the behaviour 1. Go to settings, change the font size to some nondefault value in the General Tab 2. Open About Dialog, and the dialog doesn't respect font size. Furthermore, note that some elements don't properly resize the layout upon changing the font size (and typically, an application restart is really required to make the layout fully adapt to this font size change), the screen shot below is using the WelcomeTab in development for demonstration but I think this effect is also generalizable. ### Appendix ...
Log File ``` Paste an excerpt of your log file here ```

summary: Area of preview on hover should be shrink to the size of the text displayed

description.text: Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12351 The issue was basically the box height was not getting set based on the contents of the entry. The default height was always being used. So currently I have updated the PreivewViewer.java file to get the current height based on the content of the entry. Initially I did not add any extra padding but then the last bit of the content was getting cut off. So as of now i have added extra 10 and it gives me the last bit content properly and lil extra room. I can reduce that if you want. I have fixed 70% of the issue. However still on the first render the old tooltip box height and width is being displayed but after that I am getting the correct box height and width based on the text. So after figuring out how to fix that I will change the status of the PR to "ready for review". #### Current look with extra 10 padding (both height and width: ### Steps to test ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [ ] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [/] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date?
